source.title: Peer Comparison: PDD vs Alibaba vs JD.com - Financial Metrics 2025

extractions.claim: PDD has the highest gross margin (56-61%) and operating margin (22-28%) among Chinese e-commerce peers, reflecting pure marketplace leverage
extractions.evidence: FY2025 comparison: PDD gross margin 56.3% (down from 60.9%), operating margin 21.9% (down from 27.5%). Alibaba FY2025 (ended Mar 2025): gross margin ~39.9%, operating margin ~14-15%. JD.com: gross margin ~15.9% (Q2 2025), operating margin ~2.6% (TTM). PDD's margins are structurally superior due to pure 3P marketplace + C2M model.

verification.verifier_notes: PDD from press release calculations. Alibaba from FY2025 annual report. JD.com from quarterly filings.

extractions.claim: PDD trades at lowest P/E multiple (~10x) despite highest margins, reflecting market discount for investment cycle uncertainty and geopolitical risk
extractions.evidence: Forward P/E multiples (March 2026): PDD ~10x, JD.com ~9-10x, Alibaba ~19x. PDD has highest profitability (net margin ~23%) but trades at discount to Alibaba due to: (1) investment cycle uncertainty, (2) Temu tariff/regulatory risk, (3) no capital return program (no buyback, no dividend).

verification.verifier_notes: P/E multiples from Yahoo Finance and analyst consensus as of March 2026

extractions.claim: Alibaba's take rate is recovering via 0.6% software service fee and Quanzhantui AI marketing tool, with CMR growing 12% in Q4 FY2025
extractions.evidence: Alibaba Q4 FY2025 (Mar 2025 quarter): Customer management revenue grew 12% YoY, driven by improvement of take rate from increasing penetration of Quanzhantui and addition of 0.6% software service fees. Taobao Tmall Group revenue reached RMB 101.4B in Q4 FY2025, up 9%.

verification.verifier_notes: From Alibaba FY2025 annual results press release

extractions.claim: JD.com's 1P model generates highest absolute revenue (~$187B) but lowest margins (~2.6% operating); growing 3P mix is the margin story
extractions.evidence: JD.com FY2025 annual revenue ~$187.2B (+17.9% YoY). JD Retail operating margin improved to 4.5% (Q2) and 5.9% (Q3). Consolidated operating margin ~2.6% dragged by new business investments. JD's take rate comparison is not directly applicable due to 1P gross revenue recognition vs PDD/BABA net recognition.

verification.verifier_notes: From JD.com quarterly earnings releases and MacroTrends financial data
